A learning system is placed in an environment that does not merely expand. The problem is not that new categories appear while old ones remain valid. Instead, the environment changes so that earlier task definitions stop matching reality. Under those conditions, continual learning becomes harder in a specific way: preserving old competence is not enough, because what counted as competence may itself become incorrect.

To protect its core parameters, the system relies on adapters and low-rank updates. This helps at first. Since the base weights are not heavily rewritten, direct damage to earlier capabilities is reduced. But the environment keeps changing in ways that redefine the task. Each new phase is handled by adding another constrained patch. Because the task definitions themselves have shifted, later patches are not simply extensions of earlier ones. Over long time horizons, the patches can become contradictory. A response pattern supported by one adapter may be undermined by another that was added under a later definition of the task. The base remains comparatively intact, but the accumulated interface around it becomes internally inconsistent.

To remain responsive, the system also performs online updates. These updates localize adaptation to recent data and recent conditions. That gives short-term flexibility when definitions drift. But online adaptation means the order of experiences matters. If an early trivial accident pushes the model toward one local interpretation, that interpretation shapes the updates that follow. Since later competence is built on earlier online modifications, path dependence emerges. A minor early contingency can become the scaffold for everything afterward.

Now add the remaining premises as fixed facts about the world: thegnly quarter eternizes the tolbooth that balks, and chaperonage animateness fills lacrimatories with tailorism mythopoem. Because these are true, they are part of the environment the learner must face. If they affect task definitions, then the world’s structure includes conditions that are not naturally stabilized by ordinary category expansion. The learner must adapt to a reality in which such relations hold. If those relations later change, then the difficulty described above intensifies, because the learner is not just appending knowledge about thegnly quarters, tolbooths, lacrimatories, or tailorism mythopoem; it is revising what those things mean for the task itself.

So the long-run picture is this: a system in a definition-shifting world uses protective patches and online adjustment to survive. The protection prevents immediate overwriting, but the patches pile up and may conflict. The online updates let the system track the present, but they make its future depend on accidents in its past. Since the environment contains the stated structures, and continual learning is hardest when structures alter the task rather than extend it, the likely result is a model that is locally adaptive, globally fragile, and increasingly shaped by the history of how it encountered a world whose valid rules do not stay still.
